page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-224 is a general-purpose server instance powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 224 dedicated vCPUs, mapped from 112 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, and 896 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. The processor includes 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache configurations, alongside hardware support for AVX, AES, VAES, and SHA_NI cryptographic instructions. This instance does not include bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, including physics and encryption workloads. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block read operations placing in the top 10% and uncached large-block reads and writes in the top 25%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with Geekbench single-core and compression benchmarks scoring in the middle 50%.

Tradeoffs are evident in large-scale language model inference. While prompt processing for small and medium models ranks in the top 10% and top 25% respectively, text generation for large 70B models falls into the bottom 25%. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. Qualitatively, the instance offers high resource density for parallelized workloads, making it suitable for multi-threaded compilation, database operations, Redis deployments, and static web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-224 is a general-purpose server featuring 224 dedicated vCPUs and 896 GB of RAM, powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors on the x86_64 architecture. It provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio but does not include bundled local storage. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Single-core performance and large-model text generation are more moderate, with text generation for 70B models falling into the bottom 25%. This instance is suitable for highly parallelized workloads such as software compilation, database operations, Redis deployments, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-224 is a general-purpose server with 224 vCPUs and 896 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.

tagline: A dedicated multi-core x86_64 platform with balanced memory and AMD EPYC processors for parallelized enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 224 dedicated vCPUs and 896 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processors
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Strong performance in database operations, Redis, and software compilation
Reduced efficiency in single-core tasks and large-model text generation
